_comment: Roundabout (회전). Polygon covers the ENTIRE drivable surface of the roundabout (ring-shaped roads around the centre island). Because sv.PolygonZone only supports simple polygons, draw the OUTER perimeter of the roundabout's drivable area (not a ring). The boundary-crossing filter still deduplicates correctly: a vehicle that enters, loops, and exits counts as 1. CAVEAT: ID-switching risk is higher than other road types because vehicles dwell inside longer (2-3s of looping). Expect the overcount multiplier to be ~1.3-1.5× on dense roundabouts even after filtering. Tripwire is less useful here — consider omitting it or placing at the primary entry axis.
